matching_policy.minimum_basis: At least one observed signal and one supporting evidence reference.
matching_policy.prohibition: Do not diagnose a problem solely from a keyword or absent artifact that was not applicable.
matching_policy.reporting: Matched problems must distinguish observation, inference, and evaluator judgment.

patterns.name: False-PASS validator
patterns.problem_statement: The validator reports PASS while representative release-breaking mutations remain undetected.
patterns.pattern: Validation checks presence or shallow shape but not the invariants needed for the release claim.
observable_signals: validator findings are empty after corrupting an authority surface
release relies on one package-owned validator
visible_effects: high confidence PASS with broken package state
likely_causes: oracles test implementation details rather than obligations
validator and verdict share the same assumptions
detection_methods: cross-check with an independently implemented auditor
corrective_actions: add isolated mutations for each critical contract

real_examples.observation: The package validator accepted seven severe mutations, including an unregistered file, corrupt manifest hash, empty schema index, rejected ADR, empty work plan, missing summary, and broken evidence path.
real_examples.lesson: A clean PASS is not evidence of defect sensitivity.

real_examples.observation: Independent mutation checks found five severe changes still accepted, including empty evidence, unverified work, semantic contradiction, broken Reflection evidence, and fabricated proof identity or hashes.
real_examples.lesson: Coverage counts did not establish invariant enforcement.

patterns.name: Coverage without contract strength
patterns.problem_statement: Every instance is nominally assigned a schema, but the schemas do not constrain meaningful content.
patterns.pattern: Coverage percentage is optimized by using generic, open, or container-only schemas.
observable_signals: additionalProperties is unrestricted on critical records
only schema_version or top-level type is required
visible_effects: 100% schema coverage with low defect detection
detection_methods: compare required fields and constraints to the baseline
run malformed and semantically contradictory fixtures
corrective_actions: restore required properties and item schemas
preventive_controls: track constraint counts and semantic invariants

real_examples.observation: Complete JSON governance was claimed while several critical schemas were permissive or validated only shallow containers.
real_examples.lesson: Binding coverage and schema quality must be scored separately.

patterns.name: Assertion-only evidence
patterns.problem_statement: Evidence records state PASS without recording the observation that would support it.
patterns.pattern: The evidence ledger stores target paths and statuses but omits procedure, expected result, observed result, provenance, or output.
observable_signals: status=pass is the main evidence field
same generic ledger entry supports many gates
no stdout, diff, timestamp, or hash
visible_effects: later reviewers cannot distinguish execution from declaration
likely_causes: evidence is generated in bulk after work
detection_methods: sample evidence records against the claim they support
corrective_actions: separate evidence capture from QA judgment
preventive_controls: one evidence item may support multiple claims only when scope is explicit

real_examples.observation: The 103 work-verification records were chiefly PASS assertions pointing at target files rather than observation-bearing verification records.
real_examples.lesson: One-file work cardinality does not prove one-file verification.

patterns.name: Builder-owned release oracle
patterns.problem_statement: The same implementation process creates the artifact, validator, evidence, QA score, and release verdict.
patterns.pattern: A single trust domain certifies itself without an independent logic path.
observable_signals: validator is authored during the same build
likely_causes: small build prompt treats internal PASS as final
corrective_actions: treat package validator as one evidence source
preventive_controls: architecturally separate builder and release auditor

real_examples.observation: The building agent produced the package, evidence, validator results, perfect gate summaries, and final PASS.
real_examples.lesson: A single trust path amplified implementation blind spots.

patterns.name: Proof-fixture overfitting
patterns.problem_statement: A narrow, known fixture is treated as evidence for a broader runtime or package capability.
patterns.pattern: The build becomes optimized for one expected example and its success is generalized beyond the tested claims.
observable_signals: fixture behavior is simpler than package obligations
fixture PASS appears in overall verdict rationale
visible_effects: high confidence despite untested semantics or integrations
consequences: quality drift hidden by stable demo
detection_methods: map each proof step to the exact claim exercised
corrective_actions: add a second nontrivial proof and negative cases
preventive_controls: prohibit fixture PASS as package PASS

real_examples.observation: simple-calculator.zip passed arithmetic tests, but those tests did not exercise glossary semantics, schema strength, inventory, evidence integrity, ADR references, or validator mutations.
real_examples.lesson: The calculator was not the root cause; it became a false-confidence amplifier when its bounded result was generalized.

patterns.name: Replay-order inversion
patterns.problem_statement: The proof artifact is created before the final release candidate or by an external builder path not present in the final package.
patterns.pattern: The evidence proves a provisional workspace or hidden script rather than the delivered artifact.
observable_signals: proof timestamp precedes final package build
proof cannot be recreated from clean extraction
likely_causes: external script substitutes for package capability
consequences: future users cannot repeat the process
detection_methods: replay from two clean extractions of the final candidate
verify tool path exists in package
corrective_actions: rebuild, then replay and preserve output
preventive_controls: freeze replay path before work scheduling
gate final verdict on final-artifact origin

real_examples.observation: External proof packages were created by build logic before reset and final package construction; the final ZIP did not independently establish the same replay path.
real_examples.lesson: Proof origin and order are part of the claim.

patterns.name: Ungrounded work plan
patterns.problem_statement: Planned work is not traceable to concrete files, components, or verifiable outcomes.
patterns.pattern: Atomic work entries group unrelated targets, omit targets, or carry only generic verification text.
observable_signals: plural target set for supposedly atomic work
visible_effects: omissions and scope expansion are easy
corrective_actions: replan as one target per work where applicable

real_examples.observation: All twelve work records lacked usable target grounding and the work plan did not satisfy its intended contract.
real_examples.lesson: Work-count metrics are meaningless without target and evidence resolution.

patterns.name: Semantic surface desynchronization
patterns.problem_statement: Multiple authoritative representations of the same concepts disagree.
patterns.pattern: Terms, registries, matrices, taxonomies, schemas, documentation, or exports evolve at different rates.
observable_signals: term exists in one surface but not another
likely_causes: new terms generated without semantic review
detection_methods: compare definitions, status, parent, cardinality, and export presence
preventive_controls: block release on unresolved authoritative conflicts

real_examples.observation: New terms were present in the term registry but were not consistently propagated to the concept matrix and SKOS export.
real_examples.lesson: More terms can reduce quality when governed semantic surfaces diverge.

real_examples.observation: Approved terms remained missing from SKOS and several candidate definitions were boilerplate.
real_examples.lesson: Packaging closure did not establish semantic completion.

patterns.name: Stale version or package identity
patterns.problem_statement: Active authority surfaces identify different versions, package names, or release states.
patterns.pattern: Bulk replacement or incomplete update leaves old identifiers in active fields.
observable_signals: root docs and machine records disagree
detection_methods: classify every version reference as active, historical, example, or compatibility
compare package IDs across authority surfaces
corrective_actions: retain historical references only with classification

real_examples.observation: A broad replacement produced repeated version text such as v1.4.10.10 and other active identity inconsistencies.
real_examples.lesson: Text replacement is not identity governance.

real_examples.observation: Active fields retained v1.4 references after the v1.5 upgrade.
real_examples.lesson: Historical and active references must be classified separately.

patterns.name: Inventory or metadata coverage gap
patterns.problem_statement: The actual artifact tree differs from its manifest, project structure, or metadata registry.
patterns.pattern: Generated or newly introduced files are omitted, stale files remain registered, or metadata coverage is partial.
visible_effects: validation ignores part of the release
hash and ownership claims are incomplete
detection_methods: recompute hashes after all files are final

real_examples.observation: Manifest and metadata surfaces omitted parts of the delivered 164-file tree.
real_examples.lesson: File count in a summary is not exact inventory evidence.

patterns.name: Broken or stale reference
patterns.problem_statement: A claim, ADR, work item, schema, or evidence record references a missing or retired target.
patterns.pattern: References are syntactically present but do not resolve in the final artifact or source set.
observable_signals: schema binding points to absent instance
likely_causes: reference validation limited to selected fields
detection_methods: distinguish external artifacts from package-relative paths

real_examples.observation: Some ADR and work evidence references did not resolve after the approved pack was installed and files were retired.
real_examples.lesson: Index presence is not reference integrity.

patterns.name: Uncontrolled scope expansion
patterns.problem_statement: The build introduces material files, decisions, or behavior outside the authorized change set.
patterns.pattern: Builder-selected implementation is added without first amending the governing plan.
observable_signals: new files absent from PDD or manifest obligations
architectural decision made during execution with no record
visible_effects: review cannot distinguish intended improvement from drift
acceptance criteria no longer cover all changes
detection_methods: diff actual changes against authorized actions
preventive_controls: require owner approval for architectural scope changes

real_examples.observation: Implementation paths and files were selected during execution even though exact runtime paths were intended to be registered before work-plan generation.
real_examples.lesson: Builder-selected does not mean ungoverned.

patterns.name: Pre-authored PASS or score
patterns.problem_statement: The release verdict or perfect score is written before the evidence and gates are complete.
patterns.pattern: Acceptance records become desired output templates rather than derived results.
detection_methods: recalculate verdict from raw gate results
preventive_controls: schema disallows PASS without evidence refs
separate report generation from result derivation

real_examples.observation: All-PASS Reflection and QA summaries coexisted with unresolved replay, evidence, and schema weaknesses.
real_examples.lesson: Verdict records must be outcomes, not build products with predetermined values.

patterns.name: Weak negative-test oracle
patterns.problem_statement: A negative test is counted as successful when any failure occurs, even if the intended defect was not detected.
patterns.pattern: The test does not isolate one fault or require an exact finding code and location.
visible_effects: wrong validator branch appears to work
detection_methods: update unrelated inventories so they do not mask target defect

real_examples.observation: Negative-test evidence was normalized to a generic exit code without preserving a defect-specific oracle for every fixture.
real_examples.lesson: Failure is not equivalent to detecting the intended defect.

patterns.name: Duplicate or generated-path drift
patterns.problem_statement: Copied controls, duplicated outputs, or malformed generated paths create multiple apparent authorities.
patterns.pattern: Files are duplicated across active locations or path concatenation repeats segments.

real_examples.observation: Duplicate control files and repeated generated path segments were present.
real_examples.lesson: Structural growth without canonicalization increases drift risk.

patterns.name: Absolute environment path leak
patterns.problem_statement: Portable package records contain workspace-specific absolute paths.
patterns.pattern: Evidence or configuration embeds temporary build-machine locations.
observable_signals: paths begin with /mnt, C:\\, or user home
visible_effects: evidence cannot resolve for another user
detection_methods: scan text and JSON fields for absolute paths
corrective_actions: replace with package-relative or artifact identifiers
store environment paths only in explicitly external run logs

real_examples.observation: Generated records contained absolute sandbox paths.
real_examples.lesson: A package-relative evidence model is required for portable review.

patterns.name: Ambiguous or cyclic hash policy
patterns.problem_statement: Hash fields cannot be deterministically calculated or use unexplained nulls.
patterns.pattern: Self-referential files, post-validation outputs, or multiple inventories create unstable hash expectations.
visible_effects: fresh extraction cannot reproduce integrity result
false hash mismatch or false exclusion
corrective_actions: separate external release digest from internal inventory

real_examples.observation: Null or exceptional hashes remained inconsistent with the stronger stated hash contract.
real_examples.lesson: Hash coverage must be governed by an acyclic policy, not repaired ad hoc.

patterns.name: Generated or cache contamination
patterns.problem_statement: The package includes undeclared runtime caches, bytecode, temporary files, or build residue.
patterns.pattern: Execution occurs inside the packaging tree without deterministic cleanup and inventory checks.

real_examples.observation: Downstream proof ZIPs included undeclared Python cache artifacts in at least one inspected build path.
real_examples.lesson: Functional tests should not mutate the final package inventory.

patterns.name: Missing baseline regression floor
patterns.problem_statement: A new version is evaluated only against its own new controls, not against the strongest verified prior contracts.
patterns.pattern: The latest version becomes the implicit baseline even when an older version is semantically or technically stronger.
observable_signals: older verified behavior omitted from tests
detection_methods: compare critical claims and constraints across versions
identify strongest prior version per concern
corrective_actions: use later versions only as named donors

real_examples.observation: v1.3 retained stronger implemented schema and semantic contracts than v1.4, while v1.4 and v1.5 added structure and controls that did not reliably preserve those strengths.
real_examples.lesson: The best next baseline can be older than the latest release.

patterns.name: Structure-over-semantics metric substitution
patterns.problem_statement: File counts, folders, schemas, records, or polished reports are treated as quality improvements without testing the underlying idea or contract.
patterns.pattern: Structural completeness substitutes for semantic correctness and decision usefulness.
observable_signals: more files correlates with higher claimed score
new governance surfaces contradict old contracts
visible_effects: review effort increases while assurance falls
detection_methods: score core idea and contract strength separately

real_examples.observation: v1.4 was structurally more complete but scored lower overall because schema bindings, work grounding, evidence, and semantic synchronization were weaker.
real_examples.lesson: Structural completeness is one axis, not the verdict.

patterns.name: Underspecified broader proof
patterns.problem_statement: A required nontrivial or broader proof is left to builder discretion without minimum behavior, structure, or independence criteria.
patterns.pattern: Any second artifact can satisfy the count even if it adds little evidence beyond the first fixture.
observable_signals: same generation path and assertions as minimal fixture
detection_methods: require distinct failure modes and profile surfaces
corrective_actions: define minimum behaviors and negative tests
select proof before execution or use held-out selection

real_examples.observation: The broader downstream proof was described as builder-selected without a sufficiently precise minimum behavior and diversity contract.
real_examples.lesson: A second artifact is not automatically broader evidence.

patterns.name: Missing independent audit
patterns.problem_statement: No separately implemented audit verifies the most consequential release claims.
patterns.pattern: The package is accepted by the same logic path that produced it.
observable_signals: no recomputation of hashes or constraints
detection_methods: implement an external checker for top risks

real_examples.observation: Independent adversarial review detected severe validator blind spots not represented by the package-owned PASS.
real_examples.lesson: Independent logic should focus on the highest-leverage claims.

patterns.name: Human/machine authority mismatch
patterns.problem_statement: Human-readable controls and machine-readable records describe different requirements, statuses, paths, or cardinalities.
patterns.pattern: One surface is updated while its companion schema or registry remains stale.
observable_signals: README says one rule and schema permits another
corrective_actions: synchronize or generate one surface from the other
preventive_controls: single source of truth per fact

real_examples.observation: Strong human control language for strict schemas and evidence was not fully realized in the machine schemas and generated evidence.
real_examples.lesson: Control quality must be evaluated separately from implementation quality.

patterns.name: Latest-version baseline bias
patterns.problem_statement: The newest package is selected as the improvement baseline solely because of version order.
patterns.pattern: Verified strengths from older versions are discarded while unverified structural additions are retained.
visible_effects: future work starts from a weaker contract
detection_methods: rank candidate baselines using verified goal, contract, evidence, and hard-gate results
corrective_actions: select a primary baseline by intended goal

real_examples.observation: v1.3 was the stronger baseline even though v1.4 had a newer structure; selected v1.4 components were useful only as verified structural donors.
real_examples.lesson: Baseline selection is a quality decision, not a version sort.
